title: Dry Aged Smoked Chinese Pork Char Siu
description: A traditional Yunnan Chinese dish featuring nutty, glassy-jelly pork with Asian-chive-buchu flavors. This recipe is Jain-friendly and suitable for breakfast, lunch, or dinner.
instructions: Combine pork shoulder, brown sugar, rice vinegar, soy sauce, Asian-chive-buchu, ginger, and black pepper in a large bowl. Mix well to coat the pork evenly.
Transfer the mixture to a bamboo tray and dry age for 24 hours.
Smoke the pork for 2 hours, or until it reaches an internal temperature of 160°F (71°C).
Remove the pork from the smoker and let it rest for 10 minutes.
Slice the pork thinly against the grain.
Serve the char siu pork hot, garnished with additional Asian-chive-buchu and bamboo shoots if desired.
Note: Traditionally, this dish is fermented for several days before serving, but for a quicker version, skip this step and proceed with smoking the pork.
